the bottleneck — /ultraplan moving it to browser

is the right call.

The terminal loop (plan → approve → forget context)

kills flow. Visual annotation of specific sections

would be a game changer.

Adapt

What do you think? …

Rohan Chaubey

Claude Code’s new /ultraplan feels like a real shift in how devs plan before they build.

What it is: A slash command that moves planning from the terminal into a collaborative browser experience on Anthropic’s cloud.

Problem → Solution: Planning in CLI is rigid and linear. You get a wall of text, approve/reject, and repeat.  
/ultraplan turns that into an interactive doc—comment on specific sections, iterate, and refine before writing a single line of code.

What’s different: It cleanly separates planning (cloud) from execution (local or cloud)—which just makes sense.

Key features:

results.title: VELA: Securely execute AI-generated & untrusted code | Product Hunt
results.content:
Hey Product Hunt community!

As a solo maker, I've been diving deep into the world of autonomous AI agents (LangChain, LlamaIndex, etc.). One of the biggest bottlenecks I kept hitting was code execution.

When an agent needs to analyze data, scrape a site, or run a simulation, it writes code. But where do you safely run it?

exec() or subprocess on the host machine? Terrifying. One bad prompt injection and the LLM accesses your .env files or exfiltrates data.

Standard Docker containers? Too slow and heavy for rapid-fire agent tool calls.

Cloud sandboxes? Great, but I didn't want to send my local data or proprietary agent logic to a 3rd party API just to run a simple pandas script.

2

3

Framer 3.0

What do you think? …

Praveen
Lineage Lens

Lineage Lens

David McHale [...] You're right that process isolation alone isn't enough. In VELA, isolation is enforced at the micro-VM boundary, and network access is treated as a first-class policy decision rather than an implicit default. A sandbox can be launched with networking completely disabled, which is the safest mode for many AI-generated workloads.

For cases where outbound access is legitimately required (calling APIs, downloading data, etc.), developers can grant narrowly scoped capabilities through policy and capability tokens instead of giving unrestricted access. The goal is to move from a binary "sandboxed/not sandboxed" model to a least-privilege execution model where filesystem, network, runtime limits, and auditability are all controlled per execution. [...] Florent Berrez
Foyer

Foyer

The interesting problem here isn't running untrusted code, it's what the sandbox actually contains. A lot of solutions stop at process isolation but still share a network namespace, which means a malicious payload can exfiltrate data or reach internal services even if it can't touch the host filesystem. Curious whether VELA does full network isolation per execution, and how you handle cases where the code legitimately needs outbound access, like an AI-generated script that has to hit an external API to do anything useful.

Praveen
Lineage Lens

Lineage Lens

@fberrez1 Thanks Florent — that's exactly the distinction we wanted to address.

PROCESIO

PROCESIO [...] Based on 33 reviews

Review PROCESIO?

Reviewers see PROCESIO as a powerful automation platform for people who need more control than Zapier or Pabbly typically offer. They repeatedly praise its speed, clean canvas, flexible API, webhook and SQL-based workflows, document generation, and broad use across marketing, CRM, invoicing, and backend automation. Users also highlight responsive support and a large knowledge base. The main caveat is complexity: several reviewers say it has a real learning curve and is not especially beginner-friendly, with some asking for clearer explanations of usage and execution limits.

results.title: Sentry Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Sentry is critical for Termdock and our backend services across error monitoring and performance tracing. Its issue grouping and release tracking let us map multi‑project, multi‑workspace events to the correct versions. Source Maps and Stack Traces speed up debugging, while Performance tracing visualizes slow transactions and bottlenecks. With alert rules and environment tags, we can quickly pinpoint issues in a terminal‑driven workflow and trace them back to the corresponding commit. We evaluated New Relic, Datadog, and Firebase Crashlytics. They excel at broad observability and mobile crash reporting, but we prioritize consistent frontend‑backend error context, fine‑grained release/environment segmentation, and a developer‑friendly debugging experience. As a result, we chose Sentry as [...] team needs. That structure helped us stay aligned and better manage day-to-day execution across teams. [...] Report

 9 views

•2 reviews

Sentry is a powerful error-tracking and performance-monitoring tool, available both self-hosted and in the cloud. It helps developers quickly detect, diagnose, and fix issues, improving application stability and efficiency. A must-have for seamless debugging

results.title: Tauri Competitors & Alternatives (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
5.0 ·64 reviews

Learn more →

Speed in the development loop is Vite’s core advantage, especially when Tauri isn’t the bottleneck and the frontend iteration cycle is. It starts the dev server almost instantly and keeps feedback tight with fast hot module replacement, which is a bigger win than desktop packaging improvements when UI work is changing rapidly.

Vite also stays out of the way: configuration is typically minimal, and common setups for React, Vue, Svelte, and other frameworks are handled through straightforward plugins. That makes it a strong alternative when the goal is to optimize the web build pipeline rather than adopt a desktop runtime. [...] 5.0 ·482 reviews

Learn more →

Next.js stands out when the “app” is fundamentally web-first and needs production-grade rendering options that a desktop framework like Tauri isn’t designed to solve. It brings server-side rendering, static generation, and incremental regeneration to React, which can materially improve SEO, first-load performance, and content-driven experiences.

It’s also an all-in-one platform approach: routing, data fetching patterns, API endpoints, middleware, and performance optimizations come together in one cohesive framework. That reduces the need to stitch multiple tools together for a full-stack web product. [...] Unfollow Following Follow
